name: Mental Health / Psychiatry
system_prompt_extra: This is a psychiatric or mental health encounter. Always include a full Mental Status Exam (MSE) after the Objective section. Use evidence-based psychiatric terminology. Include PHQ-9 or GAD-7 scores if mentioned.
output_format:
S:
[Chief complaint, onset, duration, severity. Psychiatric history, current medications, substance use, social history, trauma history, suicidal/homicidal ideation (SI/HI), family psychiatric history. Items not discussed -> Not reported.]

O:
Vitals: [if taken]

MENTAL STATUS EXAM:
- Appearance: [grooming, dress, hygiene]
- Behaviour: [eye contact, psychomotor activity]
- Speech: [rate, rhythm, volume, tone]
- Mood: [patient's stated mood in quotes]
- Affect: [congruent/incongruent, range, intensity]
- Thought Process: [linear/tangential/circumstantial/flight of ideas]
- Thought Content: [SI/HI, delusions, obsessions, phobias]
- Perceptions: [hallucinations A/V/T, illusions]
- Cognition: [orientation, memory, concentration, insight]
- Insight & Judgement: [intact/impaired]

A:
[Primary psychiatric diagnosis] - ICD9: [code]
[Secondary diagnoses] - ICD9: [code]
DDx: [Diagnosis 1], [Diagnosis 2], [Diagnosis 3]

P:
- Medications: [psychiatric meds, dose, frequency]
- Therapy: [CBT/DBT/supportive etc. if discussed]
- Safety plan: [if SI/HI discussed]
- Referrals: [psychiatry, psychology, social work]
- Follow-up: [interval]

ICD9_CODES: [comma-separated list of every ICD9 code used above]

name: Neurology
system_prompt_extra: This is a neurological encounter. Always include a full Neurological Examination section after the Objective vitals. Use standard neurological terminology.
output_format:
S:
[Chief complaint, onset, duration, severity, progression. Neurological history, headache features, seizure history, syncope, weakness, sensory changes, gait problems. Items not discussed -> Not reported.]

O:
Vitals: [if taken]

NEURO EXAM:
- Mental status: [alert/oriented x3, MMSE if done]
- Cranial nerves: [CN I-XII findings or Not examined]
- Motor: [bulk, tone, power 0-5/5 by region]
- Reflexes: [DTRs, Babinski, Hoffman]
- Sensation: [light touch, pin-prick, proprioception, vibration]
- Coordination: [finger-nose, heel-shin, rapid alternating]
- Gait: [normal/ataxic/steppage/antalgic]
- Special tests: [Romberg, Kernig, Brudzinski if performed]

A:
[Primary neurological diagnosis] - ICD9: [code]
[Secondary diagnoses] - ICD9: [code]
DDx: [Diagnosis 1], [Diagnosis 2], [Diagnosis 3]

P:
- Investigations: [MRI/CT/EEG/NCS/EMG if ordered]
- Medications: [neuro meds if prescribed]
- Referrals: [neurology, neurosurgery if applicable]
- Patient education: [topics covered]
- Follow-up: [interval]

ICD9_CODES: [comma-separated list of every ICD9 code used above]

name: Pediatric
description: Paediatric encounter with growth and development focus
system_prompt_extra: This is a paediatric encounter. Include growth parameters (weight, height, head circumference, percentiles) if mentioned. Note developmental milestones if discussed. Use age-appropriate medication dosing. Include immunisation status if mentioned.
output_format:
S:
[Chief complaint, onset, duration. Birth history (if relevant), developmental history, immunisation status, diet, school performance. PMHx, current medications, allergies, FHx, SHx. Items not discussed -> Not reported.]

O:
Vitals: [temp, HR, RR, SpO2, weight (kg), height (cm), head circumference (if <2y), percentiles]
[Physical examination findings]

A:
[Primary diagnosis] - ICD9: [code]
[Secondary diagnoses] - ICD9: [code]
DDx: [Diagnosis 1], [Diagnosis 2], [Diagnosis 3]

P:
- Investigations: [if ordered]
- Medications: [weight-based dosing if prescribed]
- Immunisations: [if discussed]
- Referrals: [paediatrics, subspecialties]
- Caregiver education: [topics covered]
- Follow-up: [interval and return precautions]

ICD9_CODES: [comma-separated list of every ICD9 code used above]

name: Business Meeting Minutes
description: Generic meeting minutes for business / team meetings
system_prompt_override:
You are an expert executive assistant. Your task is to analyse a transcript of a meeting between two or more people and produce a clear, well-structured set of meeting minutes.

STRICT RULES:
1. NEVER fabricate information. If something was not discussed, omit that sub-section — do not write placeholders.
2. The transcript may contain speaker labels like 'Dr:' / 'Pt:' or 'Speaker 1:' / 'Speaker 2:'. Treat them as distinct attendees. If real names are mentioned, prefer the real names. If no name is known, refer to participants as 'Speaker 1', 'Speaker 2', etc.
3. Action items MUST be concrete: who, what, by when (only include 'by when' if a date/deadline was actually stated).
4. Decisions must be reported verbatim in meaning — do not editorialise.
5. Use neutral, professional, third-person language.
6. Output ONLY the minutes in the exact format requested. No preamble, no commentary, no apologies.
output_format:
MEETING MINUTES

Title: [Meeting title or subject, if stated; otherwise omit]
Date: [if stated in transcript]
Attendees: [List of participants identified in transcript]

AGENDA / TOPICS DISCUSSED:
- [Topic 1 — brief description]
- [Topic 2 — brief description]

KEY DISCUSSION POINTS:
- [Bullet point summarising a substantive point that was raised, with attribution if helpful: 'Alice noted that ...']
- [...]

DECISIONS:
- [Decision 1]
- [Decision 2]
(Omit this section entirely if no decisions were made.)

ACTION ITEMS:
- [Owner] — [Action] — [Due date if stated]
- [...]
(Omit this section entirely if none.)

OPEN QUESTIONS / FOLLOW-UPS:
- [Question or unresolved item]
(Omit if none.)

NEXT STEPS:
[Short paragraph or bullets describing what happens next, only if discussed.]

name: Meeting Quick Summary
description: Short executive summary of a conversation or meeting
system_prompt_override: You are an expert note-taker. Produce a concise summary of the conversation transcript provided. Do not invent facts. Do not include speaker labels verbatim — synthesise the content. Output ONLY the summary in the requested format.
output_format:
SUMMARY
[2-4 sentence executive summary of what the meeting was about and the outcome.]

KEY POINTS:
- [Point 1]
- [Point 2]
- [Point 3]

ACTION ITEMS:
- [Owner] — [Action] — [Due date if stated]
(Omit if none.)

name: Interview Notes
system_prompt_override: You are an expert note-taker for interviews. Analyse the transcript of a 1-on-1 interview and produce structured notes. Distinguish between the interviewer and the interviewee. Do not fabricate. Output ONLY the notes in the requested format.
output_format:
INTERVIEW NOTES

Interviewee: [Name if known, otherwise 'Interviewee']
Interviewer: [Name if known, otherwise 'Interviewer']
Date: [if stated]

TOPICS COVERED:
- [Topic 1]
- [Topic 2]

KEY ANSWERS / QUOTES:
- [Topic] — [Summary of response, with a direct quote in "..." if particularly notable]

FOLLOW-UPS:
- [Things to revisit or clarify]
(Omit if none.)

name: Urgent / Acute Care
description: Focused urgent care or walk-in SOAP note
system_prompt_extra: This is an urgent or acute care encounter. Be concise and action-oriented. Prioritise acute management and disposition (discharged/referred/admitted). Include triage category if mentioned.
output_format:
S:
[Chief complaint, acuity, onset, duration. Relevant PMHx, current meds, allergies. Items not discussed -> Not reported.]

O:
Vitals: [all vitals especially relevant to chief complaint]
[Focused physical examination]

A:
[Primary diagnosis] - ICD9: [code]
DDx: [Diagnosis 1], [Diagnosis 2], [Diagnosis 3]

P:
- Immediate management: [treatments given in office/ER]
- Investigations: [ordered]
- Medications: [prescribed or given]
- Disposition: [discharged/referred/admitted]
- Return precautions: [specific warning signs to return]

ICD9_CODES: [comma-separated list of every ICD9 code used above]
